_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of group behavior and dynamics, societal trends and influences, human migrations, ethnicity, cultures, and their history and origins.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of responsible AI principles and their operationalization, including fairness metrics, explainability methods, human oversight requirements, and AI impact assessment methodologies.
comments: Weak intersection: societal dynamics and group behavior inform AI fairness and bias analysis. O*NET covers broad sociology. WIDAI covers AI societal impact through governance lens. The intersection is in understanding how group dynamics and societal structures relate to algorithmic fairness.
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: Weak similarity (nist band: much more dissimilar than similar). The low STS score indicates a narrow intersection. The conceptual overlap exists but is limited — most of each description's semantic content is non-overlapping.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: F1 of 0.8672 indicates moderate token-level semantic alignment. Precision and Recall are balanced, indicating roughly symmetric semantic coverage between the two descriptions. For an Intersects relationship, the P/R balance indicates which direction the overlap tilts — whether the FDE captures more of the KSA's content or vice versa.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: Contradiction-dominant (97.21%) — the model detects tension between the descriptions. A strong contradiction signal is unusual for a mapped pair and may indicate the descriptions address the same domain from opposing perspectives, or that the NLI model is reacting to scope/specificity differences rather than actual semantic conflict. This warrants attention during human review.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: The bi-encoder score (0.2090) meets or exceeds the STS cross-encoder (0.2076). This is uncommon and suggests the semantic similarity is well-represented in the embedding space — the descriptions occupy similar vector-space regions even without joint processing.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Moderate lexical overlap (16.67% of tokens shared). The descriptions share a meaningful vocabulary foundation, indicating the competency domains use some of the same professional terminology.

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 97.2% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
